item.title: Why your PCIe device isn’t detected (and it’s not a driver issue)
item.description:
Introduction – When Your First Guess is Wrong

You install a PCIe device.
You boot your system.
You run:

lspci

…and it’s not there.

Your first instinct?

“I probably need to install a driver… again.”

That’s usually the wrong conclusion.

For many modern PCIe devices, if the system can’t see the hardware, the problem isn’t in the OS at all - it starts much earlier.

Before drivers. Before Linux. Even before the OS boots.

item.title: How to write release notes
item.description:
Release notes are one of the most underrated tools in software development. Often overlooked, they can be a powerful way to communicate changes, ensure smoother deployments, and build trust across teams - and even with users.

In this post, we’ll walk through:

  • What release notes are (and what they’re not)
  • When and why to write them
  • Who benefits from them
  • What a good release note should contain
  • A practical example & reusable template

Whether you’re running a microservice architecture or a monolith, releasing internal tools or public APIs - this guide is for you.
